name: Zaynab bint Hussain
title: Namesake of Her Aunt
shortDescription: Imam Hussain's (AS) young daughter, named after her heroic aunt, who experienced the suffering of captivity in childhood.
fullStory:
Zaynab bint al-Hussain was one of Imam Hussain's (AS) daughters whose name shows the Master of Martyrs' (AS) special affection and respect for his heroic sister, Lady Zaynab al-Kubra (AS).

Witnessing the Ashura event in her childhood, she was present in the tents with her sisters (Fatima, Sakina, and Ruqayya). On the evening of Ashura, when the tents were set on fire, she too ran terrified towards the desert.

The young Zaynab was taken under the protection of her aunt Zaynab (AS) and, along with the captives' caravan, traveled the arduous route from Kufa to Sham with her small hands and feet bound in chains.

Her presence, along with other children in the captives' caravan, was always one of the tools that exposed Yazid's government, as people realized the cruel and ruthless nature of the Umayyad rule upon seeing young children in chains.
quoteArabic: وَنَحْنُ بَناتُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ تُساقُ كَما تُساقُ الأُسارَى...
quote: (Condition of the Imam's daughters:) And we are the daughters of the Messenger of God, being driven like prisoners of war...
